title: Wegner's Conjecture
statement_text: For every finite family $\mathcal{F}$ of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$, $\tau(\mathcal{F}) \leq 2\nu(\mathcal{F})$.
context_text: Wegner conjectured in 1965 that the ratio $\tau/\nu$ is bounded by $2$ for hypergraphs of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$. The best known lower bound $\tau = \lfloor 5\nu/3 \rfloor$ is due to Fon-Der-Flaass and Kostochka.
paper_contribution: Proves $\tau(\mathcal{F}) \leq c\nu(\mathcal{F})$ for special families of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$ (e.g., $r$-bounded aspect ratio in Theorem 1.10, corner intersections with a maximum matching in Corollary 1.9), establishing a constant ratio in these special cases but not the specific bound of $2$ in general.
notes: The paper's results yield $\tau/\nu = O(1)$ under additional assumptions; the conjecture for general families of rectangles with bound $2$ remains open.

title: Gyárfás–Lehel Conjecture
statement_text: For every finite family $\mathcal{F}$ of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$, the ratio $\tau(\mathcal{F})/\nu(\mathcal{F})$ is bounded by a constant.
context_text: Gyárfás and Lehel conjectured that $\tau/\nu$ is bounded by a constant for families of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$, a weaker form of Wegner's conjecture. This remains open in full generality.
paper_contribution: Proves $\tau(\mathcal{F}) \leq c\nu(\mathcal{F})$ for families of axis-parallel rectangles with $r$-bounded aspect ratio (Theorem 1.10, explicit constant $14 + 2r^2$) and for families where intersections with a maximum matching occur at corners (Corollary 1.9, constant $16$), resolving the conjecture for these special cases.

title: Weak ε-net question for axis-parallel rectangles
statement_text: Do there exist weak $\varepsilon$-nets of size $O\!\left(\frac{1}{\varepsilon}\right)$ for families of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$?
context_text: Pach and Tardos showed the smallest strong $\varepsilon$-net for axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$ has size $\Omega\!\left(\frac{1}{\varepsilon}\log\log\frac{1}{\varepsilon}\right)$; the question of whether weak $\varepsilon$-nets of size $O(1/\varepsilon)$ exist for axis-parallel rectangles was raised in both [3] (Aronov–Ezra–Sharir) and [10] (Pach–Tardos).
attributed_to: Aronov, Ezra, and Sharir; Pach and Tardos
paper_contribution: Proves existence of weak $\varepsilon$-nets of size $O(1/\varepsilon)$ for families of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$ whose aspect ratio is bounded by a fixed constant $r$ (Corollary 1.12), giving a positive partial answer.
notes: PDF source — the exact size bound in the original question may be garbled; context suggests the question concerns $O(1/\varepsilon)$ weak nets, while the known weak-net upper bound is $O(1/\varepsilon \log\log 1/\varepsilon)$.
